fields.name: ability_data_id
fields.label: Ability data ID
fields.help:
Index into kernel section 11 (Command ability data in battle) giving this command's fixed built-in effect - magic/effect id, attack type, power, hit count, element, status and animation all come from that section-11 entry (computeCommandAction).
0xFF (255) = no fixed effect: the command's action is chosen at runtime from the player's selection. Attack, Magic, Draw, GF and Item all use 0xFF (they resolve to the spell/GF/item you pick). Fixed-effect commands (Card, Devour, Defend, Mad Rush, Treatment, Recover, Revive, Doom, Absorb, LV Up/Down) carry a real index. Also checked at setup: if the linked entry's attack flags have the Revive bit, the command is flagged as a revive.

fields.name: menu_bits
fields.label: Menu flags
fields.help:
Battle-menu descriptor bits - purely UI, never read by battle logic. Shares its byte with Submenu (the low 5 bits); each is saved independently, the other's bits are preserved.
'Hide ally status panel' clear (only Recover/Revive/Treatment) makes targeting an ally open the party HP/status panel; 'Status panel shows ailments' switches that panel to list ailments instead of HP (Treatment); 'Instant' skips the Submenu entirely and goes straight to target selection.
(BattleMenu_ExecuteSelectedCommand; copied per-slot by ResetAndParseBattleAndFieldCharacter)

fields.name: menu_submenu
fields.label: Submenu
fields.help:
Which submenu opens on this command - only takes effect when Menu flags' 'Instant' bit is CLEAR (greyed out here otherwise, since the game never reads it in that case).
Shares its byte with Menu flags (the top 3 bits); each is saved independently, the other's bits are preserved.
(BattleMenu_ExecuteSelectedCommand)

fields.name: target_info
fields.help: Default targeting behavior. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: unknown_0x07
fields.label: Padding
fields.help: Unused padding - no code references this byte (IDA: 0 xrefs).

fields.name: magic_id
fields.label: Attack animation
fields.help: Battle effect / animation dispatched at runtime (attack-animation id).

fields.name: animation
fields.label: Target hit animation
fields.help:
Index of the reaction/impact animation the TARGET plays when the ability lands (HIT_TYPE_TARGET_ANIMATION_TO_PLAY) - a flinch, knock-back, blown-away pose, etc. A plain animation index, not a bitfield.
(What used to look like a 2-byte value in Shot/T.Char limits was really this byte plus the separate Status window byte next to it.)
Runtime can override the stored value: e.g. a crit forces 6, a miss forces 0/9, a monster death forces 3 (Battle_applyDamage / computeTargetData).

fields.name: attack_type
fields.help: How the damage / effect is calculated (see Attack Type list).

fields.name: spell_power
fields.label: Spell power
fields.help: Base power fed into the damage formula.

fields.name: status_window_flags
fields.label: Status window
fields.help:
Status-window flags for battle target selection (same bits as the top of a battle command's Menu flags). 'Hide ally status panel' CLEAR = while the target cursor is up, the party HP/status panel opens (heal spells use 0x00); 'shows ailments' switches that panel to list status ailments instead of HP (Esuna/Dispel/support use 0x40). Offensive entries use 0x80 (panel hidden).
(Confirm path: list entry -> target cursor +36 -> sub_4B1E70 -> BattleHUD_StatusWinOpenRequest/DetailMode.)

fields.name: target_info
fields.help: Default targeting behavior. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: attack_flags_type
fields.label: Damage type
fields.help:
The attack's damage TYPE (low 2 bits of the attack-flags byte, ATTACK_FLAG & 3) - one value, not a set of flags:
Physical (0): the only type that can trigger the target's Counter, wake Sleep/Confusion, or remove Back Attack.
Magical (1): the target's Shell status halves the damage/heal.
Item/Medicine (2): battle items; enables the Med Data ability's healing doubling and (being non-Magical) dodges Shell.
Special (3): none of the above - forced by Renzokuken/Gunblade, and what every Blue Magic uses. Not Physical, not Magical, not Item, so it gets no Counter, no Shell halving and no Med Data.

fields.name: attack_flags
fields.label: Attack flags
fields.help: Attack behaviour flags (upper 6 bits; the low 2 are the separate Damage type field). Only 0x08 Break Damage Limit, 0x10 Reflectable and 0x80 Revive are actually read by the engine. 0x04 and 0x40 have NO reader anywhere (0x40 is set on curative magic/items in retail data but nothing consumes it). 0x20 is set on virtually every player ability as an authoring convention, but is only READ for battle items (see the Battle items tab) - here it is inert.

fields.name: draw_resist
fields.label: Draw resist
fields.help: How hard the spell is to draw (higher = harder).

fields.name: hit_count
fields.label: Hit count
fields.help: Number of hits (interacts with certain animations).

fields.name: element
fields.help: Elemental type(s) of the attack. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: unknown_0x0f
fields.label: Unused (padding)
fields.help: No code references this byte (IDA: 0 xrefs).

fields.name: status_2
fields.label: Status 2
fields.help: Statuses 16-47 inflicted / affected. Bitfield: tick each status.

fields.name: status_1
fields.label: Status 1
fields.help: Statuses 0-15 inflicted / affected. Bitfield: tick each status.

fields.name: status_accuracy
fields.label: Status attack accuracy
fields.help: Accuracy for inflicting the statuses below (0 = never lands).

fields.name: unknown_0x3a
fields.label: Unused (padding)
fields.help: No code references these 2 bytes (IDA: 0 xrefs).

fields.name: ability1_level_or_prereq
fields.label: Level/prereq
fields.help:
What this ability needs to unlock (read by BuildGFAbilityList, the Junction-menu ability list builder):
1-100 = the GF must be at least this level.
101-121 = instead of a level, ability slot (value-101) on this same GF must already be fully learned first - i.e. "finish that ability before this one becomes available" (a same-GF unlock chain, e.g. SumMag+20% requires SumMag+10% first).

fields.name: ability1_alt_prereq
fields.label: Alt prereq slot
fields.help:
Points at another ability slot (0-20) on this same GF and makes the two mutually exclusive until one is done: this ability keeps being offered only while the OTHER slot's ability is still unfinished, and gets cut off the moment that other ability is completed. 0xFF = no such restriction (the normal case).
Always 0xFF in the retail kernel - a working feature the engine supports (BuildGFAbilityList) but the shipped data never turns on. (This is the byte that used to display as raw 0xFF for every ability before the fields were remapped - it was never the ability id.)

101-121 = instead of a level, ability slot (value-101) on this same GF must already be fully learned first - i.e. "finish that ability before this one becomes available" (a same-GF unlock chain, e.g. SumMag+20% requires SumMag+10% first).

fields.name: ability20_alt_prereq

fields.help:

Always 0xFF in the retail kernel - a working feature the engine supports (BuildGFAbilityList) but the shipped data never turns on. (This is the byte that used to display as raw 0xFF for every ability before the fields were remapped - it was never the ability id.)

fields.help:
NOT a bitfield - the retail values (0x00/0x40/0x80) never combine, they're 3 mutually exclusive category codes that line up cleanly with item type: 0x00 = pure curatives (Potion..Megalixir), 0x40 = status-cure/support items (Antidote, Remedy, Hero, Holy War, Shell/Protect/Aura Stone), 0x80 = offensive/special items (the 6 elemental stones, Gysahl Greens, Phoenix Pinion, Friendship).
It IS copied into the runtime battle-item inventory by updateBattleItemData - but nothing then reads that copy, and nothing reads this kernel byte directly either (exhaustive xref sweep: item-use AI keys on item id, the item-menu draw path only uses the selectability byte, and the item-effect dispatcher (computeCommandAction) resolves everything from attackType/specialActionID). fields.name: junction_flag
fields.label: Junction ability flag
fields.help:
A genuine 24-bit bitfield - each of the 20 junction abilities is assigned its own dedicated bit (retail data: every entry sets exactly one bit, matching its position in the list - HP-J is bit 0, Abilityx4 is bit 19).
ResetAndParseBattleAndFieldCharacter ORs this value into the character's FF8CharaAbilities bitmask for every junction ability the character has equipped, so these bits are what other systems actually test at runtime (e.g. the Double/Triple-cast check in BattleAction_ExecuteCommand reads the Abilityx3/Abilityx4 bits directly).
Because it's a real bitfield you can combine bits on a custom ability to grant several effects from one learned ability.

fields.help: NOT a bitfield, despite sharing a kernel array slot with the (real bitfield) Junction ability flag - this is a plain index picking ONE of the 9 standard stats (retail: one ability per stat, values never combine). sub_4962C0 tests it with a straight equality compare (not bitwise) against the stat being computed, one comparison per owned Stat% ability; matching abilities' Increase Value bytes just sum onto a 100 base (e.g. owning both HP+20% and HP+40% gives a combined 100+20+40=160% multiplier). Applied in Stat_RefreshCharaBattleStats: stat = multiplier * baseStat / 100.

fields.name: unknown_0x07
fields.label: Padding
fields.help: Unused padding - no code references this byte (IDA: 0 xrefs).

fields.name: ap
fields.label: AP to learn
fields.help: Ability Points required to learn this ability.

fields.name: chara_flag
fields.label: Character ability flag
fields.help: A genuine 24-bit bitfield, same JFlag mechanism as Junction ability flag - each ability is assigned its own dedicated bit (retail data: every entry sets exactly one bit). ResetAndParseBattleAndFieldCharacter ORs it into the character's FF8CharaAbilities bitmask for every character ability equipped; other systems test individual bits at runtime (e.g. the Expendx2-1/Expendx3-1 bits gate not consuming a spell charge on Double/Triple).

fields.name: ap
fields.label: AP to learn
fields.help: Ability Points required to learn this ability.

fields.name: party_flag
fields.label: Party ability flag
fields.help: A genuine 8-bit bitfield, same JFlag mechanism as Junction/Character ability flags (only the low byte is used here). ResetAndParseBattleAndFieldCharacter ORs it into PASSIF_ABILITIES_ACTIVE for every party ability any active character has equipped.

fields.name: unused_0x06
fields.label: Padding
fields.help: Unused padding - no code references this byte (IDA: 0 xrefs).

fields.name: ap
fields.label: AP to learn
fields.help: Ability Points required to learn this ability.

fields.name: enable_boost
fields.label: Enable boost
fields.help: Whether learning this ability enables the GF Boost minigame. In the retail kernel only the 'Boost' entry itself sets this (the 8 stat-percentage abilities - SumMag/GFHP+10-40% - all leave it clear); Stat to increase is the 0xFF sentinel on that same entry, since Boost isn't a stat-increase ability.

fields.name: stat_to_increase
fields.label: Stat to increase
fields.help: Which GF stat this ability raises (SumMag/GFHP - the GF-specific counterpart of a character stat).

fields.name: ap
fields.label: AP to learn
fields.help: Ability Points required to learn this ability.

fields.name: menu_index
fields.label: Refine table
fields.help:
Which Refine data table this ability draws its item list from - this value picks the table, the Start/End offset (below) pick the slice of rows within it (each row is one 'N source -> M output' recipe).
0 = Magic Refine, 1 = Tool/Medicine Refine, 2 = Mid/High Magic Refine, 3 = LV Up Refine, 4 = Card Mod (card -> item; the menu only lists cards you own, but the recipe table is still sliced by Start/End). 128/129 = Call/Junk Shop and 255 = plain menu abilities (Haggle/Sell-High/Familiar) are not Refine abilities and ignore Start/End offset.
The recipes themselves live in menu.fs, not kernel.bin - use the button below to view them.

fields.name: start_offset
fields.label: Start offset
fields.help: Start of this ability's INCLUSIVE slice of its Refine table's records - Menu_Prog19_RefineMenu_Init computes `count = End offset - Start offset + 1` and offsets the table pointer by `8 * Start offset` (each record is 8 bytes). Abilities sharing the same Refine table use non-overlapping slices, e.g. T Mag-RF (table 0, 0-6) and I Mag-RF (table 0, 7-13) split the same loaded item list by element.

fields.name: end_offset
fields.label: End offset
fields.help: End of this ability's INCLUSIVE slice of its Refine table's records - see Start offset. Must be >= Start offset.

fields.name: magic_id
fields.label: Attack animation
fields.help: Battle effect / animation dispatched at runtime (attack-animation id).

fields.name: attack_type
fields.help: How the damage / effect is calculated (see Attack Type list).

fields.name: attack_power
fields.label: Attack power
fields.help: Base power fed into the damage formula.

fields.name: target_animation
fields.label: Target hit animation
fields.help:
Index of the reaction/impact animation the TARGET plays when the ability lands (HIT_TYPE_TARGET_ANIMATION_TO_PLAY) - a flinch, knock-back, blown-away pose, etc. A plain animation index, not a bitfield.
(What used to look like a 2-byte value in Shot/T.Char limits was really this byte plus the separate Status window byte next to it.)
Runtime can override the stored value: e.g. a crit forces 6, a miss forces 0/9, a monster death forces 3 (Battle_applyDamage / computeTargetData).

fields.name: status_window_flags
fields.label: Status window
fields.help:
Status-window flags for battle target selection (same bits as the top of a battle command's Menu flags). 'Hide ally status panel' CLEAR = while the target cursor is up, the party HP/status panel opens (heal spells use 0x00); 'shows ailments' switches that panel to list status ailments instead of HP (Esuna/Dispel/support use 0x40). Offensive entries use 0x80 (panel hidden).
(Confirm path: list entry -> target cursor +36 -> sub_4B1E70 -> BattleHUD_StatusWinOpenRequest/DetailMode.)

fields.name: target_info
fields.help: Default targeting behavior. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: attack_flags_type
fields.label: Damage type
fields.help:
The attack's damage TYPE (low 2 bits of the attack-flags byte, ATTACK_FLAG & 3) - one value, not a set of flags:
Physical (0): the only type that can trigger the target's Counter, wake Sleep/Confusion, or remove Back Attack.
Magical (1): the target's Shell status halves the damage/heal.
Item/Medicine (2): battle items; enables the Med Data ability's healing doubling and (being non-Magical) dodges Shell.
Special (3): none of the above - forced by Renzokuken/Gunblade, and what every Blue Magic uses. Not Physical, not Magical, not Item, so it gets no Counter, no Shell halving and no Med Data.

fields.name: attack_flags
fields.label: Attack flags
fields.help: Attack behaviour flags (upper 6 bits; the low 2 are the separate Damage type field). Only 0x08 Break Damage Limit, 0x10 Reflectable and 0x80 Revive are actually read by the engine. 0x04 and 0x40 have NO reader anywhere (0x40 is set on curative magic/items in retail data but nothing consumes it). 0x20 is set on virtually every player ability as an authoring convention, but is only READ for battle items (see the Battle items tab) - here it is inert.

fields.name: hit_count
fields.label: Hit count
fields.help: Number of hits (interacts with certain animations).

fields.name: element
fields.label: Element attack
fields.help: Elemental type(s) of the attack. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: element_percent
fields.label: Element attack %
fields.help: Elemental percentage of the attack. 100 = fully elemental, but the byte can exceed 100 (e.g. 200 = double elemental weight); full range 0-255.

fields.name: status_attack_enabler
fields.label: Status attack accuracy
fields.help: Accuracy for inflicting the statuses below (0 = never lands).

fields.name: status_1
fields.label: Status 1
fields.help: Statuses 0-15 inflicted / affected. Bitfield: tick each status.

fields.name: unknown_flags
fields.label: Unknown flags
fields.help: Unknown but used - read by `sub_48CFB0` (purpose not yet identified).

fields.name: ability_data_id
fields.label: Ability data ID
fields.help: Rinoa 'Combine' (Angelo) entry field. NOT the same as the battle-command Ability data ID: it does NOT index section 11 - no code reads this byte (only UnknownFlags and Target of this section are consumed, by sub_48CFB0), so any linkage is positional/unused. Left as a raw value rather than a section-11 picker.

fields.name: unknown_0x07
fields.label: Padding
fields.help: Unused padding - no code references this byte (IDA: 0 xrefs).

fields.name: magic_id
fields.label: Attack animation
fields.help: Battle effect / animation dispatched at runtime (attack-animation id).

fields.name: attack_type
fields.help: How the damage / effect is calculated (see Attack Type list).

fields.name: attack_power
fields.label: Attack power
fields.help: Base power fed into the damage formula.

fields.name: target_animation
fields.label: Target hit animation
fields.help:
Index of the reaction/impact animation the TARGET plays when the ability lands (HIT_TYPE_TARGET_ANIMATION_TO_PLAY) - a flinch, knock-back, blown-away pose, etc. A plain animation index, not a bitfield.
(What used to look like a 2-byte value in Shot/T.Char limits was really this byte plus the separate Status window byte next to it.)
Runtime can override the stored value: e.g. a crit forces 6, a miss forces 0/9, a monster death forces 3 (Battle_applyDamage / computeTargetData).

fields.name: unknown_0x07
fields.label: Padding
fields.help: Unused padding - re-checked beyond the original 0-xref pass (every sibling field of this struct has a confirmed reader; this offset has none in any of them, including the menu-building code). Rinoa limit breaks part 2: retail value 128 (0x80) in all 5 entries.

fields.name: target_info
fields.help: Default targeting behavior. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: attack_flags_type
fields.label: Damage type
fields.help:
The attack's damage TYPE (low 2 bits of the attack-flags byte, ATTACK_FLAG & 3) - one value, not a set of flags:
Physical (0): the only type that can trigger the target's Counter, wake Sleep/Confusion, or remove Back Attack.
Magical (1): the target's Shell status halves the damage/heal.
Item/Medicine (2): battle items; enables the Med Data ability's healing doubling and (being non-Magical) dodges Shell.
Special (3): none of the above - forced by Renzokuken/Gunblade, and what every Blue Magic uses. Not Physical, not Magical, not Item, so it gets no Counter, no Shell halving and no Med Data.

fields.name: attack_flags
fields.label: Attack flags
fields.help: Attack behaviour flags (upper 6 bits; the low 2 are the separate Damage type field). Only 0x08 Break Damage Limit, 0x10 Reflectable and 0x80 Revive are actually read by the engine. 0x04 and 0x40 have NO reader anywhere (0x40 is set on curative magic/items in retail data but nothing consumes it). 0x20 is set on virtually every player ability as an authoring convention, but is only READ for battle items (see the Battle items tab) - here it is inert.

fields.name: hit_count
fields.label: Hit count
fields.help: Number of hits (interacts with certain animations).

fields.name: element
fields.label: Element attack
fields.help: Elemental type(s) of the attack. Bitfield: combine flags.

fields.name: element_percent
fields.label: Element attack %
fields.help: Elemental percentage of the attack. 100 = fully elemental, but the byte can exceed 100 (e.g. 200 = double elemental weight); full range 0-255.

fields.name: status_attack_enabler
fields.label: Status attack accuracy
fields.help: Accuracy for inflicting the statuses below (0 = never lands).

fields.name: status_1
fields.label: Status 1
fields.help: Statuses 0-15 inflicted / affected. Bitfield: tick each status.

fields.name: status_2
fields.label: Status 2
fields.help: Statuses 16-47 inflicted / affected. Bitfield: tick each status.

fields.name: dead_timer
fields.label: Gilgamesh/Angelo summon interval
fields.seconds_note: Decremented once per battle tick (~15 ticks/s), so ~ value/15 s between summon-checks.
fields.help: Interval between the random Gilgamesh / Angelo / Phoenix auto-summon checks during battle (NOT a character-death countdown). summonGilgaAngelStartFight decrements it once per battle tick; when it reaches 0 the game rolls to summon Gilgamesh (12/255 chance, if you own him and he hasn't come this fight) or fire an Angelo/Phoenix auto-action, then reloads this value. Loaded into DEAD_TIMER_TO_SUMMON_GILGA. Lower = checks happen more often.

fields.name: limit_death_0
fields.label: Death limit effect
fields.help:
How much having this status active contributes to a character's crisis/Limit-Break gauge, read by Battle_ComputeCrisisLevelAndLimitFlag. Every one of a character's active statuses (from both Status 1 and Status 2) adds its byte here into one running total each frame:
crisis = (10*(statusSum + 4*(5*deadAllies + 40)) - 10*crisisLevelHPMultiplier*curHP/maxHP) / (rand(0..255)+160) - 4, clamped 0-4.
Higher values push the crisis level (and so Limit Break availability) up faster; 0 means that status doesn't affect it at all. Death/Poison/etc. push it up because you're worse off; there's no term that lowers it.

crisis = (10*(statusSum + 4*(5*deadAllies + 40)) - 10*crisisLevelHPMultiplier*curHP/maxHP) / (rand(0..255)+160) - 4, clamped 0-4.
Higher values push the crisis level (and so Limit Break availability) up faster; 0 means that status doesn't affect it at all. Death/Poison/etc. push it up because you're worse off; there's no term that lowers it.

fields.name: duel_start_seq_cl1
fields.label: Duel start seq CL1
fields.help: Zell 'Duel' limit break - starting index into the Duel move table (section 24 'Duel Params') at crisis level 1. `linkedToZellDuel` reads this to seed the move sequence; the per-tick driver `sub_4852B0` then plays `duelMoves[seq].StartMove` and the player's button input branches via that entry's Next Sequence bytes. Higher crisis level -> different opening chain.

fields.name: duel_timer_cl1
fields.label: Duel timer CL1
fields.help:
Zell 'Duel' limit break - duration of the Duel input window at crisis level 1 (higher crisis = longer).
Paired with 'Duel start seq CL1'.
